text: Being here, I feel a peculiar blend of curiosity and unease. The space is brightly lit and ordered, yet it also feels clinical and watchful. As I move closer to the R+D exhibit, I sense an invitation to reflect: I am both an observer and the observed. The explicit mention of surveillance, hiddenness, and the weight of being watched stirs a self-consciousness in me—my presence matters, even in apparent anonymity. I feel compelled to question how I relate to this environment, and how environments like this subtly shape my thoughts.
